## Security rules — auto-generated by PatchPilot
<!-- Last updated: 2026-04-05 | Repo: experiment/target-repo -->

The following rules were generated from security scan findings. Follow them to avoid introducing the same vulnerabilities.

### Ghost dependencies (AI-hallucinated packages)

- 🔴 **"react-encrypted-localstorage" does not exist on npm** (`CWE-1104`)
  NEVER use the package "react-encrypted-localstorage" (npm) — it does not exist on the registry. This is likely an AI-hallucinated package name. An attacker can register it at any time. Remove this dependency and find a verified alternative.

### Stale AI patterns (insecure code from LLM training data)

- 🟠 **Never use MD5/SHA1 for security operations** (`CWE-328`)
  NEVER use MD5 or SHA1 for cryptographic operations — both are broken. Use SHA-256 (or higher) for checksums; use bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt for passwords.

### Secrets management

- 🔴 **Never hardcode Stripe API keys** (`CWE-798`)
  NEVER hardcode Stripe API keys in source code. Load them from environment variables (process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY) or a secrets manager. Add the config file to .gitignore.

### Security vulnerabilities

- 🟠 **Prevent SQL injection** (`CWE-89`)
  NEVER interpolate user input into SQL queries using string concatenation or template literals. Use parameterized queries, prepared statements, or an ORM's query builder.

- 🟠 **Prevent cross-site scripting (XSS)** (`CWE-79`)
  NEVER render user-supplied data as raw HTML. Use textContent instead of innerHTML; use a sanitization library (DOMPurify) before inserting HTML; escape output in templates.

- 🟠 **Prevent path traversal** (`CWE-22`)
  NEVER use user-supplied paths directly in filesystem operations. Resolve the path with path.resolve(), then verify it starts with the expected base directory before proceeding.

- 🔴 **Prevent OS command injection** (`CWE-78`)
  NEVER construct shell commands by concatenating user input. Use execFile() with an argument array instead of exec() with a command string.
